steps.title: Compare benefit and belief
steps.content: The passage states that volunteering benefits society overall, but the survey shows most youths recognize only individual benefits. Only 6% said volunteering helps fix broader social problems, so many young people are missing part of volunteering's value.

steps.title: Reject unsupported shifts
steps.content: A shifts to where youths think they can volunteer, which the survey does not discuss. C ranks societal benefits above individual benefits, but the passage never compares their size. D shifts to finding opportunities, which is also outside the survey. The logical point is that volunteering has more value than many youths recognize. The answer is B.
